text: fat is 3g. the fat quality is doing a lot of the work here. fat is basically not the story here.
carbs is 81.5g. the carb side gets messy pretty quickly. for grains, the carb quality matters much more than the raw number.
protein is 7g. protein quality is a real strength here. protein helps, but not enough if the carb side is weak.
vitamin e at 1% DV stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.
minerals are basically not part of the case here.

the upsides first. portable and convenient because very easy to use as a quick snack base. easy to portion and carry because simple packaging advantage. works with many toppings because practical flexibility for snack content.

on the weaker side. does not justify taking up your grain slot because other grain foods usually bring much more actual nutritional value. usually needs help to feel worthwhile because it often relies on toppings or pairings to become a decent snack. not very filling for the calories because can be unsatisfying unless built up with other foods.
D tier. Best only as a light snack base or texture food, not as a strong staple by itself.
